content.full_text:
July 15, 1992

John Wallace
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Dear John:

I would like the Commission’s ruling on the appropriateness of Commission for Economic Development (CED) Chair Leo McCarthy’s involvement in the production of an educational video by the California not-for-profit organization, Vital Options, Inc. The video, A Personal Touch: A Guide to Self-Exam, is designed to educate young adults about self-examination and early detection of breast and testicular cancer. The CED’s role in the project would be to raise funds from the private sector to help underwrite the project; distribute the video through public schools, community colleges and universities; and raise public awareness about the video through the media.

The CED has a history of working with the private sector on projects benefiting the state’s economic development. Health care education is an integral part of a strong economy. Sections 14999.7 (c) and (e) of the Government Code state that the CED has the authority to cooperate with public and private organizations to carry out its powers and duties and in so doing, may accept donations and grants to this end.

Vital Option’s mission is to meet the psychological, emotional, informational and resource needs of young adults with cancer between the ages of 17 and 40. The not-for-profit agency sponsors support groups, offers referral services and educational services for health care providers serving younger cancer patients. All services are provided without charge.

The CED will not play a dominant role in the day-to-day production of the video, nor will the commission extend its involvement to Vital Option’s other operations. Neither the Lt. Governor nor any member of his family have any ties to Vital Options. All funds raised will go directly into the production of the video. The CED will retain some control over decisions governing expenditures of the funds raised for the video, but Selma Schimmel, president of Vital Options, will retain the dominant role in these matters.

We are tentatively planning to have the Lt. Governor's name and title appear at the end of the video, along with a listing of sponsors. His name would appear as follows: Lt. Governor Leo McCarthy, Chair, Commission for Economic Development.

Your review of this case would be appreciated. If you have any questions, please don't hesitate to call me at 445-8994.

Sincerely,

Ann C. Mills
Issues Director
